figures.caption: Fig. 7: Tactile sensing and shape mapping performance across various end-effectors. Comparison of raw sensing inputs (i) and physical rendering outputs on TAG (ii) across various robot end-effectors (right). TAG effectively translates diverse sensor geometries into coherent tactile feedback: (a) LeapTac: linear ridge; (b) Inspire Hand: circular area; (c) XHand: dual contact points; (d) GelSight Mini and (e) TacSL: complex shapes.
TABLE II: Results of multimodal desk lamp control.

Fig. 2: Hardware architecture of TAG. (a) Exploded view of the joint encoder, utilizing a magnetometer and ring magnet for angle sensing. (b) Exploded view of the tactile feedback module, a multi-layer EOP stack sandwiching a membrane between PCBs. (c) TAG is compatible with diverse arm tracking solutions, such as 6DoF trackers or exoskeletons. (d) Kinematic configuration distributing 21 encoders for full-hand motion capture.
Fig. 15: Illustration of Filament Pinching and Pull-out Task. The schematic shows the thumb and index finger pinching the filament.

Fig. 10: Real-world teleoperation tasks using TAG. (a) Multimodal lamp control: Hierarchical interaction via short press, press-and-turn, and subtle rotation. (b) Sequential filament pinching: Distinguishing successful capture of a 1.75mm filament from empty pinches via localized tactile feedback. (c) Multi-level spring compression: Maintaining three distinct pressure levels (0.5N, 3.5N, 7.5N) guided by rendered tactile feedback.
Fig. 4: Dynamic demonstration of the TAG feedback module. (a) Active area expansion in Pressure Mode under varying pressure levels. (b) Dynamic force profiles simulating forward-to-backward motion.
Fig. 14: Illustration of teleoperation tasks in Press Mode. (a) Multimodal Control of a Smart Desk Lamp. (b) Multi-level Spring Compression.

Fig. 5: Exoskeleton glove tracking performance. (a) Experimental setup for joint-tracking. (b) Real-time tracking results for discrete and continuous trajectories. (c) Long-term stability test over 1000s of operation. (d) Statistical distribution of instantaneous tracking errors with Gaussian fit.
